w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: AI-Integrated Management System with SMS Reminder for House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming Salon –
In Partial Fulfillment of the Requirements for
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming was established by Dr. Analiza P. Yorac, DVM, and located

providing veterinary and grooming services for pets in the local community. The facility offers a range of services, including routine medical check-ups, vaccinations, laboratory examinations, prescription management, and pet grooming. Its mission is to ensure the health and well-being of pets while delivering convenient and high-quality services to pet owners.

Currently, the clinic relies on a paper-based system to record and manage patient information. This system documents client and owner details, pet information, reasons for visits, clinical observations, prescriptions, and follow-up reminders. While functional, this manual approach requires additional time for data recording, retrieval, and verification, especially as the number of patient records grows.
Certain circumstances have revealed the limitations of the current system, particularly regarding record security and continuity. Environmental

factors have previously affected stored records, resulting in the loss of important patient information. Preserving records for extended periods also requires significant storage space and careful handling to prevent damage or misplacement. Additionally, retrieving patient information from physical files can be time-consuming, especially during busy clinic hours, which can affect service delivery and client satisfaction.

Prescription management and follow-up reminders further illustrate the need for improved efficiency. Handling prescriptions involves multiple steps to ensure accuracy and proper association with patient records. Follow-up notifications for vaccinations, laboratory procedures, and consultations also demand careful coordination to maintain client compliance. Enhancing these processes can improve the clarity, organization, and accessibility of information, thereby supporting more consistent and effective care.

To address these challenges, this study proposes the development of an Artificial Intelligence-Integrated Management System with SMS reminders for House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming. The proposed system aims to improve data accessibility, record organization, and the accuracy of stored information. It also seeks to reduce retrieval and documentation time, strengthen client follow-up through automated notifications, and enable secure management of patient records and
prescriptions. Through these improvements, the clinic can enhance operational efficiency and ensure continuity of care for pets.
Review of Related Literature, Studies, and Systems

systems to be accountable, meaning that there should be mechanisms in place for tracing decisions back to the organizations and individuals responsible. AIMS framework emphasizes the importance of ethical considerations in AI management. It provides a structured approach to integrating ethical principles, such as fairness and accountability into AI systems. Transparency is achieved through clear documentation, open communication, and stakeholder engagement, ensuring that AI operations are understandable and accessible to all relevant parties. This approach ensures that AI technologies evolve in response to emerging challenges and opportunities, maintaining their alignment with ethical standards and organizational objectives. It ensures that AI systems are developed and deployed with a consistent focus on ethical use, transparency, and accountability

The necessity of such systems is theoretically anchored in the Information Systems Success Model (ISSM), which asserts that the utility of an information system is determined by the convergence of system quality, information quality, and service quality (Peters, 2025). In the specific case of veterinary clinics, system quality refers to the security and availability of cloud-based storage, which acts as a safeguard against the permanent data loss associated with paper records (
, 2025). Information quality is realized through the organization and digitization of records, ensuring they are searchable and retrievable in real-time

The Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) provides a complementary perspective by focusing on user adoption (DLSU, 2022). TAM posits that the actual use of a system is a direct function of the users' behavioral intention, which is influenced by two primary factors: perceived usefulness and perceived ease of use (Peters, 2025). For the staff at House of Puppies, the perceived usefulness of the system will be tied to its ability to automate reminders and simplify prescription management, while ease of use will depend on the intuitiveness of the user interface
Deep Learning and Clinical Decision Support

Advancements in Deep Learning (DL), a subfield of artificial intelligence, have introduced algorithms that simulate the problem-solving capabilities of the human mind (Frontiers in Veterinary Science, 2025). The literature describes how Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) have become the gold standard for image analysis in veterinary medicine, excelling at identifying hierarchies of spatial features in radiographs and pathology slides (Frontiers in Veterinary Science, 2025). These systems are increasingly proficient in recognizing and classifying cell types, demonstrating versatility across diagnostic modalities

Parallel to DL, the literature on digital Clinical Decision Support (CDS) tools highlights their role in promoting evidence-based therapeutic decisions (Frontiers in Veterinary Science, 2024). These tools emulate the veterinarian's thought process by integrating patient-specific data with broader clinical knowledge, which is particularly vital for antimicrobial stewardship (Frontiers in Veterinary Science, 2024). By providing pop-up reminders and automated data entry, CDS tools reduce the cognitive load on practitioners, thereby decreasing the likelihood of medical errors
Ethical Principles in Non-Human Patient AI

The ethics of veterinary AI are unique due to the "client-patient-practitioner" relationship (AI & Society, 2024). Literature identifies nine distinct ethical issues, including data security, trust, and the moral status of the animal patient (AI & Society, 2024). Because animals lack the same legal
as humans, they are potentially more exposed to algorithmic

(AI & Society, 2024). The "Four Rs" principles—Reduction, Refinement, Replacement, and Responsibility—are often cited as a guide for ethical decision-making in animal experiments and are being adapted for digital contexts

The governance of health data is a global concern. International guidelines emphasize the need for privacy protection while allowing for the mining of data for research purposes (OECD, 2021). This is particularly relevant as AI systems require vast amounts of high-quality data for training (Al-
et al., 2024). In a veterinary context, this involves balancing the clinic's need for diagnostic accuracy with the owner's right to data confidentiality

The concept of mobile health (mHealth) has redefined the client-practitioner interface (BMJ Global Health, 2021). SMS reminders have demonstrated a positive impact on attrition-prone healthcare services, such as vaccination schedules and follow-up check-ups (MDPI, 2024). Literature indicates that caregivers often delay or miss appointments due to a lack of awareness regarding due dates, a challenge that is effectively mitigated by the global availability of mobile phones

The categorization of digital technologies provides a taxonomy for the current technological shift (Zaki, 2019). Literature sources define digital transformation as the extent to which a firm utilizes digital technologies to develop new value-adding business models (Verhoef et al., 2021). In the veterinary sector, this manifests as a transition from reactive care to proactive, data-driven health management
According to official statistics, MSMEs comprise 99.5% of the total number of businesses in the country (Government of the Philippines, 2023).

Reports highlighted that a significant 70.6% of these businesses were forced to temporarily shut down during the early months of the pandemic because they were unable to cope with the business-related impacts of COVID-19 (BMU, 2024). This crisis catalyzed a mandatory shift toward digitalization, as firms adopted social media and e-commerce platforms to remain competitive

Literature from economic development banks indicates that internet or e-commerce use did not lead to immediate positive outcomes in 2020, but by 2021, a clear positive impact emerged as firms developed the "digital maturity" necessary to harness these tools (ADB, 2021). This delayed impact suggests that for House of Puppies, the implementation of a management system must be accompanied by a period of organizational learning and staff training
The Philippine government has implemented regulations to enhance animal health and welfare, primarily through the Animal Welfare Act (Philippine Congress, 1998). This legislation
that veterinary establishments comply with standards for facility licensing and record-keeping (Shubham, 2025). The adoption of veterinary software is promoted
support compliance and accurate documentation, thereby
encouraging clinics to integrate modern technologies for better patient care

Furthermore, the Philippine Development Plan 2023-2028 specifies digital transformation as an underlying theme to make the country "globally competitive" (Government of the Philippines, 2023). The goal is to bridge the "digital divide" in a nation where 40% of the population still lacks reliable internet access (Government of the Philippines, 2023). Reports have indicated over 114.6 million mobile connections, suggesting that SMS remains the most accessible channel for client communication

A substantial cultural shift has occurred in the Philippines, where pets are no longer viewed solely as working animals but as cherished family members (Abao et al., 2024). This holistic approach to pet care has fueled the demand for professional services, including grooming and advanced medical care (Abao et al., 2024). However, pet owners continue to experience "pain points" in accessing professional veterinary services and accurate medical information (Abao et al., 2024). Local literature suggests that there is a clear "market fit" for digital platforms that offer a centralized
location for pet health records and service booking

Resilience is defined as the ability to resist, absorb, and recover from the effects of a hazard efficiently (UNISDR, 2020). For Philippine businesses, this involves "emergency preparedness" and "crisis planning" (Sanchis et al., 2020). Literature specifically addressing micro-enterprises notes that while many businesses are knowledgeable about disaster risks, they often lack the technical infrastructure to ensure business continuity (Dales et al., 2020). The use of cloud-based systems is identified as a critical "gain creator" for these businesses, allowing them to bounce back quickly after a disruptive event
Despite the clear benefits, several barriers to digital adoption persist in the Philippines. These include cultural resistance, a lack of technical

struggle with the cost and complexity of digital tools (Philippine Institute for Development Studies, 2019). Regional areas are even more constrained by infrastructure and regulatory issues, creating a "digital divide" between urban and rural settings

Local literature on telehealth has demonstrated its effectiveness during the pandemic as an alternative to in-person consultations (DLSU, 2022). The outpatient services utilized virtual triage through phone calls and SMS to manage outpatient flows, highlighting areas for improvement such as the lack of protocols for documenting electronic medical records (DLSU, 2022). These findings underscore the need for the proposed House of Puppies system to include a robust "Notification Module" and systematic "Client/Patient" records
Systematic Review of Deep Learning in Diagnostics

A systematic review utilized the PRISMA guidelines to analyze the intersection of deep learning and veterinary medicine, identifying 422 relevant research articles (Frontiers in Veterinary Science, 2025). The study found that AI models are frequently "at least as good" as human healthcare experts in specific diagnostic tasks (Frontiers in Veterinary Science, 2025).
proficiency in recognizing and counting cell types on microscope slides suggests it can significantly augment the diagnostic
capabilities of small clinics like House of Puppies
Efficacy of SMS Reminders in Rabies Control

Research conducted in rural Kenya evaluated the efficacy of SMS reminders in improving adherence to the five-dose rabies Post-Exposure Prophylaxis (PEP) regimen (Masika et al., 2022). The study involved a field trial comparing a control group with an intervention group that received SMS reminders one day before their scheduled doses (Washington State University, 2022). The results showed that the odds of PEP completion were 3.37 times higher among those who received the SMS reminder (Masika et al., 2022). Furthermore, the intervention group had better compliance with the scheduled dates, with a mean deviation of only 0.18 days compared to 0.79 days for the control
Performance of AI-Driven Clinical Decision Support

A study on the Veterinary Hospital Management System (VHMS) using an AI-integrated advisory found that rule-based decision-making systems effectively analyze symptoms and history to suggest potential diagnoses (Rao & Srinivasan, 2023). This feature is particularly useful for reducing diagnostic errors and supporting junior physicians (Rao & Srinivasan, 2023). The architecture allows for the incremental addition of
machine learning algorithms based on historical treatment records to enhance its suggestions over time

A study evaluated the satisfaction of pet owners using the "VET PROMPT" notification system via mobile applications (PMC, 2025). The statistical analysis yielded a "High" level of satisfaction, with an overall mean score of 4.16 (PMC, 2025). Specific metrics highlighted the system's "Ease of use" (4.25) and "Ease of understanding" (4.20)
. Notably, the system's reliability was confirmed with
Alpha of 0.97, indicating exceptional consistency across its components

A meta-analysis of 25 studies evaluated the effectiveness of Mobile Phone Text Message Reminders (MPTMRs) on vaccination coverage. The evidence supports the use of MPTMRs for improving vaccination uptake compared to usual care, with a risk ratio of 1.09. The study concluded that intervention characteristics and country setting had no bearing on the effectiveness, suggesting that SMS reminders are a universally applicable tool for veterinary wellness programs.
AI Adoptions in the Veterinary Sector

A descriptive analysis of 812 published works up to March 2023 categorized the major areas of AI implications in the veterinary field. The largest category was "Diagnostic Studies" (192 studies), followed by animal production, epidemiology, and health and welfare. The analysis showed that machine learning (ML), CNNs, and deep learning (DL) were the primary methodologies, with DL being referred to 46.83% of the time.

Research in rural Haiti found that while inconsistent cellular signals and lack of phone access were barriers, 22.2% of patients reported they would not have attended their visits without the SMS reminder. This feasibility study demonstrated that manual or automated SMS composing is acceptable to patients and can increase appointment attendance rates in diverse clinical settings.
Studies on e-health cloud computing systems highlight their ability to decrease healthcare service costs while augmenting services through real-
time data sharing between departments. Cloud systems are particularly praised for their "agility" and "portability,"
clinical patient data from various sources (medication records, lab data, claims) to be centralized for collaborative care.
AI vs. Human Experts in Image Interpretation

Research indicates that AI tools for diagnostic imaging are often 100 times faster in providing results compared to humans. Digital pathology is noted as being more accurate than humans when evaluating high-resolution slides. However, veterinarians still play a critical role in linking AI findings to the patient's holistic clinical background before making final decisions.

The integration of IoT with AI models has been shown to predict health issues early. For example, odor sensors can predict ketosis in dairy cattle, while motion and sound sensors can detect mastitis and foot-and-mouth disease. In companion animals, smart collars have been used to generate "overall health scores," providing clinicians with continuous data for evaluation.
Telehealth Evaluation in Metro Manila and Regions III/IV-A
A significant local study evaluated five commonly used telehealth applications:
. The research utilized the Telehealth Usability Questionnaire (TUQ) and found that

was the only application that exceeded the population mean in every usability factor. The findings revealed no significant difference between the gender of respondents and their ratings, though female participants generally gave slightly higher scores.

The "Groom Master" study evaluated a web-based pet grooming appointment scheduling and inventory management system. Developed using the Agile SDLC, the system centralizes online booking, inventory tracking for supplies like shampoos and conditioners, and customer preference records. The study found that the iterative development process, based on user feedback from groomers and receptionists, significantly improved modules like pet profiles and scheduling alerts.
SMS and MMR Immunization in Rural Philippines

A comparative descriptive study was conducted to determine the effectiveness of text messages in improving Measles, Mumps, and Rubella (MMR) immunization compliance in rural areas of the Philippines. The study found that text messaging, with or without pictures, is a feasible and useful tool. Parents who received text reminders brought their children for immunization on a "timelier basis" compared to those who received verbal reminders alone.
SMS Reminders for GDM Postpartum Care at PGH

A single-blind randomized controlled trial at the Philippine General Hospital (PGH) compared SMS reminders to usual care for Gestational Diabetes Mellitus (GDM) patients. The study reported a follow-up rate of 71.6% among those who received persistent SMS reminders. This study highlighted that given the low cost of text messaging in the Philippines (approximately PHP 1.00), a text-based system is a highly culture-appropriate and effective strategy for ensuring continuity of care.

A study focused on the Iris Veterinary Clinic in Cavite City examined the transition from manual to automated patient information management. The researchers found that the manual system led to inefficiencies in searching patient records and monitoring inventory. The proposed automated system successfully demonstrated the ability to generate accurate sales reports and maintain updated inventory levels, thereby increasing competitive advantage.
Research presented to the Polytechnic University of the Philippines (PUP) discussed the development of a computerized management system for
Animal Clinic. The study addressed the limitations of manual processes that
index cards and logbooks. The "Notification Module" was evaluated as a key feature that allowed the clinic to notify customers automatically via SMS based on established pet protocols.
Palawan Pet Owner Pain Points Survey

A study in Puerto Princesa City, Palawan, characterized pet owners' pain points in sourcing products and accessing professional services. The survey of 100 pet owners revealed that 93.0% faced information-seeking
barriers. The findings provided a basis for the introduction of "
," a digital platform that owners prioritizing health, medical care, and grooming expressed a willingness to pay for.
Priority Scheduling in the I-Vet Management System

The I-Vet system study explored the use of a "Priority Scheduling Technique" to address problems with manual appointment systems. The technique assigns priority levels based on the severity of a pet's injury, ensuring that those in critical need are treated first. The system was found to facilitate better time management for both staff and pet owners.
Camacho Veterinary Clinic Online Ordering Evaluation

A capstone project evaluated an online ordering and appointment system for the Camacho Veterinary Clinic. The study aimed to manage appointment requests and solve queries related to product availability. The researchers used a modified waterfall model and found that the integrated system effectively managed order details and pickup schedules.
Digital Innovation impact on MSME success
A mixed-methods study examined how digital innovation helps the entrepreneurial success of Philippine MSMEs. The study identified that cultural

and a lack of technical expertise were leading obstacles. However, the research suggested that when structural barriers are removed, digital tools significantly enhance the resilience and competitiveness of small firms.

Provet Cloud is a cloud-based practice management solution designed for clinics of all sizes and specialties. Its primary differentiation is its "user-friendliness" and a comprehensive suite of features including client communications, digital whiteboards, and business intelligence reporting. Users highlight its simple and reliable interface as one of the best in the market, particularly praising the responsive and knowledgeable customer support

is a robust, cloud-based platform popular among general practices and specialty referral hospitals. It is "jam-packed" with tools for optimizing workflows, collecting and processing data, and capturing charges automatically. Its integration with IDEXX
PLUS provides a unified platform for diagnostics, reducing manual entry and errors.

IDEXX Neo is designed specifically for ease of use and accessibility, making it an excellent choice for startups or clinics aiming to minimize IT overhead. The interface ensures that team members can get up to speed quickly, with built-in training that claims to make common tasks five times faster. Its strength lies in its tight integration with the broader IDEXX ecosystem.
Shepherd is an AI-powered cloud-based system designed by veterinarians specifically for veterinarians. It leverages "

," a medical record transcription tool that listens to live conversations and auto-fills SOAP notes in real-time, intelligently filtering out noise. This focus on reducing administrative burnout makes it a favorite among practices seeking a straightforward solution.
is a cloud-based platform with a focus on AI-enabled tools and client experience. Its "Tails AI" virtual assistant automatically creates patient summaries and SOAP
assists with record navigation. The platform includes a client-facing mobile app that enhances engagement and satisfaction through easy booking and chat features.

is a cloud-based management system built in Asia, for Asia. Since 2020, it has supported the digital transformation of clinics like Hope Veterinary Clinic and Visayas State University (VSU). It manages appointments, patient records (EMR), and inventory with localized support that understands the specific tax and health regulations of the region. A key feature is its integration with Zoetis blood test machines, allowing results to flow directly into the system
The I-Vet management system, explored in several local contexts, performs record handling for pet information, medical histories, and
vaccines. It provides a fast and accurate system for medicine inventory and generates reports for monitoring needs.
-based architecture allows for ease in tracking patient records and client waiting times across different clinic branches.

House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming manages patient records, prescriptions, and client follow-ups as part of its daily operations. As the number of clients and transactions continues to increase, there is a need to enhance operational efficiency, improve data accessibility, and strengthen service delivery, while reducing processing time, staff workload, and missed follow-ups or scheduled return visits. These improvements are essential to support continuous and quality pet care.

House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming Salon faces challenges in efficiently managing patient records, prescriptions, and follow-ups, resulting in high processing time, heavy staff workload, and missed vaccinations or scheduled return visits.
Patient records are at risk of loss or damage due to the lack of secure and reliable storage, which threatens the continuity of medical histories.
Retrieving patient records is time-consuming, taking 4-7 minutes per record, due to misplaced and non-alphabetized logbooks, which
Staff spend significant time on manual documentation and follow-up tracking, consuming 1–2 hours per day, leading to increased workload and decreased productivity.
Prescriptions are written and recorded manually, taking 15–30 minutes to process all patient prescriptions, with 10–15% sometimes lost or illegible, affecting accuracy and traceability.
Follow-ups, vaccinations, and scheduled return visits are tracked manually, resulting in approximately 25% of clients missing their appointments or vaccinations.

To develop an Artificial Intelligence-integrated management system with SMS reminders that will increase efficiency, data accessibility, and accuracy, while decreasing processing time, staff workload, and missed follow-ups or scheduled return visits, thereby improving clinic operations and continuity of pet care.
To increase data security and reliability through secure digital storage and automated backups, reducing the risk of data loss.
To reduce record retrieval time to under one minute, improving accessibility of patient information.
To reduce staff workload and increase productivity by cutting time spent on documentation and follow-up tracking by at least 50%.
To decrease prescription processing time and improve accuracy and traceability,
all prescriptions are properly recorded and easily retrievable.
To improve follow-up compliance and reduce missed vaccinations or scheduled return visits from 25% to under 5% using automated SMS notifications.
This study is anchored on the Information Systems Success Model (ISSM) developed by

and McLean (1992; updated in 2003) and the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) proposed by Davis (1989). These complementary models provide a robust foundation for evaluating both the effectiveness of the proposed AI-integrated management system and its acceptance among clinic staff. According to ISSM, the success of an information system depends on three quality dimensions: System Quality, Information Quality, and Service Quality. In the context of House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming Salon, System Quality refers to the system’s technical performance, including fast and reliable operation, secure cloud-based storage, and automatic backup mechanisms that prevent permanent

data loss. Information Quality emphasizes the accuracy, completeness, and timely availability of digitized patient records, vaccination schedules, prescription histories, and medical data, ensuring staff have reliable information for decision-making. Service Quality reflects the system’s user-friendly interface, automated prescription generation, SMS reminders for pet owners, and ongoing technical support, which enhance usability and operational efficiency.

While ISSM explains how system quality drives organizational outcomes, TAM addresses how users accept and adopt new technology. TAM proposes that two key perceptions—Perceived Ease of Use and Perceived Usefulness—influence user adoption. In this study, Perceived Ease of Use refers to clinic staff’s perception that the system is intuitive, easy to navigate, and requires minimal training, while Perceived Usefulness reflects their belief that the system improves job performance by reducing manual documentation, speeding up record retrieval, and streamlining processes such as appointment scheduling, prescription management, and follow-up reminders. Perceived Ease of Use positively influences Perceived Usefulness, and both constructs impact Actual System Use, which in turn affects User Satisfaction. Staff who perceive the system as both useful and easy to use are more likely to adopt it in daily operations, leading to improved workflow and more efficient service delivery.

By integrating ISSM and TAM, this study presents a comprehensive framework linking system inputs, processes, and outputs as illustrated in the IPO diagram. Improvements in system, information, and service quality are expected to enhance staff perceptions of ease of use and usefulness, driving consistent system utilization, higher user satisfaction, and measurable organizational benefits such as reduced errors, faster record retrieval, secure patient data, and enhanced continuity of pet care. Feedback from system use and user satisfaction further supports continuous refinement of the AI-integrated system, ensuring it becomes increasingly reliable, responsive, and tailored to the clinic’s evolving needs, thereby sustaining both system effectiveness and user adoption over time.
Identify problems (manual records, data loss, slow retrieval, missed follow-ups)
Define objectives (digitization, automation, AI integration, SMS reminders)
Determine system scope (patient records, prescriptions, reminders, reporting)
Collect data from clinic staff and operations
Analyze current manual processes (recording, retrieval, prescriptions, reminders)
Identify inefficiencies (delays, errors, data loss risks)
Design system architecture and centralized database
Create user interface for clinic staff
Plan system features (patient records, e-prescriptions, SMS reminders, AI tools)
Design system architecture and centralized database
Create user interface for clinic staff
Plan system features (patient records, e-prescriptions, SMS reminders, AI tools)
Test SMS notification accuracy and timing
Validate data storage, retrieval, and linking of records
Fix errors and improve system performance
Continuously update features and improve reliability
Identify problems (manual records, data loss, slow retrieval, missed follow-ups)

Collect data from clinic staff and operations

Identify inefficiencies (delays, errors, data loss risks)
Design system architecture and centralized database
Create user interface for clinic staff
Plan system features (patient records, e-prescriptions, SMS reminders, AI tools)
Design system architecture and centralized database
Create user interface for clinic staff

Test SMS notification accuracy and timing

Fix errors and improve system performance

Faster record retrieval (seconds instead of minutes)
Automated SMS reminders for vaccinations and follow-ups
Electronic prescription management linked to patient records
Reduced data loss and improved record security
Improved workflow efficiency and reduced staff workload
Accurate, organized, and easily accessible clinic data
Reduced missed appointments and improved continuity of pet care
Faster record retrieval (seconds instead of minutes)
Automated SMS reminders for vaccinations and follow-ups
Electronic prescription management linked to patient records
Reduced data loss and improved record security
Improved workflow efficiency and reduced staff workload
Accurate, organized, and easily accessible clinic data
Reduced missed appointments and improved continuity of pet care
This IPO diagram presents the structured development of a Web-Based AI-Assisted Management System with SMS
using the Agile Model, an iterative and flexible software development approach. It is divided into three main components
with a feedback loop to support continuous refinement and system enhancement.
The input section defines the essential resources and prerequisites required to develop the system:
Web development frameworks and tools such as Next.js (React 19)
Database management using Prisma with SQLite
Programming and data handling languages (TypeScript, SQL, JSON, Markdown)
Next.js v16.1.5 for frontend and backend integration
Desktop or laptop with at least Intel Core i3 or Ryzen 3 processor
Active cellular connection for SMS delivery
The system development follows the Agile Model, consisting of iterative phases that allow continuous improvement and flexibility:
Identify existing problems such as manual record-keeping, data loss, slow retrieval, and missed follow-ups
Define system objectives including digitization, automation, AI integration, and SMS reminders
Determine the scope of the system (patient records, prescriptions, reminders, and reporting)
Collect data from clinic staff and daily operations
Analyze current manual workflows such as recording, retrieval, prescriptions, and reminders
Identify inefficiencies including delays, errors, and risks of data loss
Develop system architecture and centralized database structure
Design an intuitive user interface for clinic staff
Plan system features such as patient records, e-prescriptions, SMS reminders, and AI tools
Implement system architecture and database integration
Build the user interface and core functionalities
Integrate features like patient management, electronic prescriptions, SMS notifications, and AI assistance
Validate SMS notification accuracy and timing
Ensure proper data storage, retrieval, and record linking
the system within the clinic environment
Continuously enhance features and maintain system reliability
Web-Based AI-Assisted Management System with SMS
, offering the following features and benefits:
Secure and centralized digital patient record system
Faster data retrieval, reducing processing time significantly
Automated SMS reminders for vaccinations and follow-ups
Electronic prescription management linked to patient records
Reduced data loss and improved data security
Enhanced workflow efficiency and reduced staff workload
Organized, accurate, and easily accessible clinic data
Decreased missed appointments and improved continuity of care

A feedback mechanism connects the Output back to the Process, allowing continuous system evaluation and improvement. User feedback and system performance data are used to refine features, fix issues, and ensure the system remains effective, efficient, and aligned with clinic needs.
Scope and Delimitation of the Study

The study focuses on the design and development of a Web-Based AI-Integrated Management System with SMS Reminder functionality for House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming Salon located at 149 Malaria Rd.,
. 185 Malaria, North Caloocan City, Metro Manila 1428.

The system aims to digitize and organize clinic records within the veterinary clinic, including pet owner information, pet profiles, medical history, vaccination records, treatment details, and prescription history to ensure accurate and well-managed documentation.
clinic staff to register, update, and manage client and pet information using a secure digital database.
veterinary staff to record and update pet medical information, vaccination schedules, and treatment history for easier monitoring and retrieval.
veterinary staff to create digital prescriptions that are directly linked to the pet and client profiles, with the option to save and export
automatically sends notifications to pet owners regarding vaccination schedules, follow-up visits, and laboratory check-ups to improve appointment compliance and continuity of pet care.
clinic staff to quickly search, access, and retrieve patient records and prescription histories to support efficient clinic operations.
Allows the system to generate reports related to prescriptions, client profiles, and patient records to assist clinic staff in monitoring clinic activities.

be conducted within House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming Salon to assess the system’s functionality, usability, reliability, and effectiveness in improving record management and reducing manual workload.

The system is strictly delimited to patient record management and administrative operations; therefore, it excludes features for medical diagnosis, treatment recommendations, or AI-based clinical decision-making.

The SMS reminder module is designed as a one-way communication tool dependent on existing cellular network infrastructures, with the study focusing on the system's ability to trigger alerts rather than guaranteeing real-time delivery in areas with poor signal reception.

The study is restricted to the development of an internal management interface for authorized clinic staff only, intentionally excluding the creation of external portals or self-service modules for pet owners.

specifically for the unique operational workflows of House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming Salon; as such, compatibility with multi-branch systems or integration with external veterinary clinics is considered beyond the scope of this study.
The system is developed as a web-based platform that requires active internet connectivity for database synchronization, as the
implementation of an offline-mode architecture was excluded from the current project design.
This study is important for several groups, including owners, admin, staff, customers, technology developers, and future researchers.
By making the AI-Integrated Management System with SMS Reminder, the research aims to establish a
that streamlines appointment scheduling, client communication, and record management.
The system enhances efficiency and decision-making by providing organized records and automated reminders, reducing missed appointments, and improving overall business performance.
It minimizes manual workload and scheduling errors, allowing staff to focus more on delivering quality veterinary and grooming services.
It ensures timely care for pets through SMS reminders for vaccinations, grooming, and check-ups, while improving convenience and strengthening trust in the clinic's services.
rovides a model for improving clinic operations through an AI-Integrated Management System with SMS reminders. It can help similar
businesses enhance appointment management, client communication, record keeping, and overall service efficiency.
The project serves as a practical case study for integrating AI and SMS technology into small-to-medium enterprises, offering a
that can be adapted to other industries.
The study serves as a practical reference for students in developing AI-integrated systems with SMS features, enhancing their technical skills and understanding of real-world system implementation.

This study contributes to academic literature by demonstrating the application of AI-driven management systems in specialized service sectors, providing a foundation for further exploration and innovation.

This study provides a practical framework for designing and implementing an AI-Integrated Management System with SMS reminders in small-to-medium service businesses. It can serve as a reference for developing similar systems, improving automation, client communication, and AI-driven features in future projects.
The extent to which users actively utilize the proposed system for managing records, scheduling appointments, sending SMS reminders, and performing clinic operations.
The authorized personnel who manages the system, maintains client and pet records, schedules appointments, sends SMS reminders, and generates reports.
A system feature that processes clinic data, automates appointment scheduling, and supports SMS reminder generation and reporting.
A system configuration that incorporates Artificial Intelligence features to automate scheduling, manage records, and enhance clinic operations.
The proposed system developed in this study that manages clinic records, automates scheduling, and generates SMS reminders for veterinary and grooming services.
The system process of performing tasks such as scheduling and reminder sending without manual intervention.
The users’ willingness to use the proposed system for managing clinic operations and appointments.
A system structure where clinic data and records are stored and managed in one unified platform.
A system setup where clinic data is stored and accessed through an online server instead of local storage.
The individual or team responsible for designing, building, and maintaining the proposed system.
The process of converting manual clinic records into digital format within the system.
to guide the development and evaluation of the proposed system.
The visual layout of the system where users interact to manage records, appointments, and services.
The accuracy, relevance, and reliability of data generated and stored by the system.
The digital platform used to manage clinic records, appointments, and service monitoring.
The evaluation model used in this study to measure system quality, information quality, service quality, user satisfaction, and net benefits.
The overall improvements in efficiency, service delivery, and record management gained from using the system.
A system accessible through a web browser using an internet connection.
The degree to which users believe the system is easy to operate and navigate.
The degree to which users believe the system enhances clinic performance and efficiency.
The file format used by the system to generate downloadable and printable reports.
The level of support and assistance provided by the system to meet clinic operational needs.
The text messaging feature used by the system to send appointment reminders to clients.
The performance, reliability, and functionality of the proposed system.
The theoretical framework used in this study to evaluate users’ acceptance of the system based on perceived usefulness and ease of use.
The system design that allows users to easily understand and operate its features.
The authorized clinic personnel who interact with the system to manage records and services.
The level of contentment experienced by users after utilizing the system.

This chapter outlines the methods used to analyze, design, and develop the proposed Web-Based AI-Integrated Management System with SMS Reminders for House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming. It includes an assessment of the clinic’s current record management practices, the proposed technological solution, and the technical components involved in building the system. The methodology followed in this study ensures that the system is designed to meet the specific operational needs of the clinic, particularly in managing patient records, prescriptions, and follow-up reminders efficiently.

The main problem addressed by this study is the inefficient management of patient records, prescriptions, and follow-up reminders at House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming, where all records are documented manually using logbooks and printed forms. This traditional record-keeping method has resulted in several operational issues, including slow retrieval of patient records, increased staff workload, misplaced prescriptions, and missed follow-up appointments. The clinic also

previously experienced the loss of important patient records due to a typhoon, which highlighted the vulnerability of paper-based documentation to environmental hazards and the need for a more secure and reliable record management system.

To address these challenges, the study proposes the development of a Web-Based AI-Integrated Management System with SMS Reminders. The proposed system aims to digitize patient records, automate prescription management, and send automated reminders to pet owners regarding vaccinations and follow-up visits. By implementing these features, the system is expected to improve the efficiency of clinic operations, reduce the workload of clinic staff, and ensure better continuity of care for pets by maintaining accurate and accessible medical records.

To better understand the current situation and determine the need for a technological solution, the researchers conducted an initial investigation and feasibility study. This process involved analyzing the clinic’s existing workflow and evaluating the limitations of the manual documentation system currently being used. The results of the investigation indicated that a web-based digital management system could significantly improve the organization and retrieval of patient records, minimize the risk
of data loss, and streamline the clinic’s daily administrative and medical documentation processes.

To gather the necessary data for the study, the researchers utilized interviews, questionnaires, and direct observations at the clinic. These data-gathering methods allowed the researchers to understand the daily responsibilities of the clinic staff and identify the specific challenges they encounter when managing patient records manually. Interviews were conducted with six staff members, consisting of two veterinarians, three veterinary assistants, and one groomer, to obtain detailed insights into their experiences with record documentation, prescription writing, and follow-up reminder management.

During the observation process, it was noted that all patient records are documented using logbooks and printed forms, which include pet owner information, pet profile and identification details, purpose of visit (such as check-ups, vaccinations, or grooming), chief complaints, laboratory test waivers, sedation consent forms for grooming, surgery consent forms, and clinical records with treatment history. Questionnaires distributed to the six staff members further revealed that documentation tasks require approximately one to two hours per day, while retrieving a patient record

takes four to seven minutes due to the lack of a structured filing system. The data collected from these interviews, observations, and questionnaires were essential in identifying the weaknesses of the current manual system and in defining the functional features needed for the proposed AI-integrated management system.

Currently, House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming utilizes a manual paper-based record management system to document patient information and manage clinic operations. This method has been used since the clinic was established and remains the primary system for recording patient visits, prescriptions, and medical history.

Under this system, clinic staff manually record all patient information in logbooks and printed forms during each visit. These records include the pet owner’s information, the pet’s details, the reason for the visit, and the clinical findings recorded by the veterinarian. Additional documents such as laboratory test waivers, grooming sedation consent forms, and surgery consent forms are also filled out manually and stored physically in the clinic.
Prescriptions are written by hand by the attending veterinarian and attached to the patient’s record. To retrieve previous prescriptions or clinical
records, staff members must manually search through logbooks and stored documents, which often requires four to seven minutes for a single record.

Follow-up reminders for vaccinations, consultations, and laboratory tests are also handled manually. A staff member sends individual SMS reminders to pet owners, which usually takes approximately thirty minutes per day. Despite these efforts, the clinic still experiences missed follow-up appointments due to the absence of an automated reminder system.
Although the manual system allows the clinic to document patient information and maintain basic records, it presents several operational challenges:
Patient records are not digitally organized, requiring staff to manually search through logbooks and files, which slows down the service process.
Paper records are vulnerable to environmental hazards. A previous typhoon destroyed several patient records, resulting in the permanent loss of important medical histories.
Manual documentation, prescription writing, and reminder tracking consume a significant portion of staff time, reducing efficiency during clinic operations.
Handwritten prescriptions can sometimes be difficult to read or misplaced, which may lead to potential treatment errors.
Since reminders are sent manually, some pet owners fail to receive timely notifications, resulting in missed vaccinations and follow-up consultations.

Despite these limitations, the clinic has continued using the manual system because it is familiar to staff and does not require additional technical resources or software investment. However, as the number of patients continues to grow, the limitations of the manual system have become more evident.

These challenges highlight the need for a digital management system that can securely store records, automate reminders, and allow clinic staff to quickly retrieve patient information. The proposed Web-Based AI-Integrated Management System with SMS reminders is intended to
address these limitations and significantly improve the efficiency and reliability of clinic operations.

Based on the Input-Process-Output (IPO) model, the proposed system for House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming shows how the needed resources are used to develop an improved clinic management system. This model explains how inputs are transformed into a system that helps manage patient records, prescriptions, and follow-ups more efficiently.

needed to build the system. These include the required knowledge in web development tools such as Next.js, TypeScript, and database management using Prisma with SQLite. User interface tools like Tailwind CSS and

UI are also included to make the system easy to use. In addition, the system requires a Windows 11 operating system, computers or laptops for clinic staff, internet connection through a modem or router, and a printer for documents.
These inputs are based on the current problems of the clinic, such as manual record-keeping, slow retrieval of patient records, risk of
, and missed follow-ups. These issues highlight the need for a more efficient and reliable system.
The system is developed using the
. In the planning phase, the problems in the current system are
such as digital record management, automation, AI

, and SMS reminders are defined. In the requirements gathering phase, information is collected from clinic staff, and the current processes are studied to understand delays, errors, and inefficiencies.
In the design phase, the system structure and database are planned, and a simple and user-friendly interface is created for the staff. The
of the system include digital patient records, electronic prescriptions, automated SMS reminders, and AI-assisted tools.

During the development phase, the system is built based on the design. After that, testing is conducted to make sure the system works properly. This includes checking if records can be saved and retrieved quickly, if prescriptions are
SMS reminders are sent correctly. Errors are fixed to improve the system.
Finally, the system is deployed in the clinic and continuously improved based on user feedback.

The final output is a web-based AI-assisted management system with SMS reminders. This system provides secure storage of patient records, faster access to information, automated follow-up notifications, and organized prescription management. It also helps reduce staff workload, prevent data loss, and minimize missed appointments, leading to better and continuous pet care.

ethods approach, combining both quantitative and qualitative data to evaluate the proposed AI-assisted management system with SMS reminders for House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming. The research focuses on improving the management of patient records, prescriptions, and follow-ups, aiming to enhance efficiency, reduce staff workload, and minimize missed appointments. The participants of this study are the

six clinic staff members who handle patient records, prescriptions, and follow-up tracking. Their experiences and feedback will be used to assess the system’s functionality, usability, and overall impact on clinic operations.

Data will be collected through observation and survey/questionnaires. Observations will measure task completion times, workflow efficiency, and accuracy of record-keeping before and after system implementation. Surveys and questionnaires will gather qualitative data on staff satisfaction, system usability, and perceived improvements in daily operations. The study will use ISO 25010 as

instrument to evaluate system quality. Key attributes such as functional suitability, usability, reliability, security, and efficiency will be measured. Staff will rate system usability using a simple Likert scale, while quantitative data such as record retrieval time, missed follow-ups, and error rates will be analyzed to determine overall system effectiveness.

Collected data will be treated statistically to provide clear results. Quantitative data such as task completion time and missed appointments will be presented as averages and percentages, while qualitative survey responses will be

summarized to show patterns and insights regarding system usability and staff satisfaction. Ethical considerations are strictly observed in this study. All participants’ information will be kept private and confidential, and informed consent will be obtained before data collection. Staff participation is voluntary, and no personal identifiers will be disclosed in any reports or documentation. This research design ensures a comprehensive evaluation of the system, combining measurable improvements in clinic operations with staff perceptions to provide a clear picture of its effectiveness and usability.
To collect information on system usability, efficiency, and reliability, the study will employ a planned survey questionnaire for clinic staff. This questionnaire will evaluate staff

of the AI-assisted management system with SMS reminders, focusing on usability, satisfaction, and confidence in performing daily tasks such as managing patient records, prescriptions, and follow-ups. A 5-point Likert
5 = strongly agree) will be used to quantify responses.
An observation checklist will be used to record staff interactions with the system during task performance. This will include measuring task completion times,
the accuracy of patient records and prescriptions. These observations will provide quantitative data on system efficiency, reliability, and functional suitability.

The system will be evaluated using ISO 25010 software quality standards to ensure that key attributes, including functionality, usability, reliability, security, and efficiency, meet established quality criteria. These tools together ensure that the results capture both actual user experiences and measured system performance, providing a complete assessment of the system’s effectiveness in improving clinic operations.
to measure the clinic’s current problems and evaluate improvements after the system is implemented. Percentages,
averages, and simple comparisons will be used to present results clearly.
All patient records were lost due to a typhoon. Using the formula:
Total records lost ÷ Total records available × 100

All six staff members handling records experience delays because files are misplaced and not alphabetized. Using:

lost or unreadable. Using the formula:
The percentage of affected prescriptions is
Out of 40 scheduled follow-ups per month, 10 are missed. Using:
Manual tasks delay staff 1–2 hours per day.
from staff comments and open-ended survey questions will be summarized into
To ensure that this study is conducted responsibly and ethically, the following considerations were observed:
All patient and clinic data, including medical records, prescriptions, and staff feedback, are treated as strictly confidential. Access to data is limited to authorized personnel only.
Names and identifying information of the clinic staff are not disclosed in the questionnaires, observations, or any reports. Staff responses are kept anonymous to ensure honesty and privacy.
All data collected in this study will be used solely for research and development of the AI-assisted management system with SMS reminders for House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming.
The research team assumes full responsibility for managing the data, protecting participants, and ensuring that the study and system development are conducted ethically.
After system deployment, appropriate guidance and training will be provided to clinic staff to ensure the system is used responsibly, efficiently, and ethically.

Prior to collecting feedback, all participants will receive a clear explanation of how their data will be used, stored, and protected. Informed consent will be obtained from all staff participants before any data collection begins.

This information model illustrates the flow of data and interactions within the House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic and Grooming Salon, focusing on how client, pet, and service-related information is managed across different roles. Clients provide essential data such as personal information, pet details, requested services, billing, and payment data, which are handled and processed by the clinic staff.

The staff serves as the central hub of the system, coordinating information between clients, veterinarians, groomers, and logbooks. Veterinarians receive and update pet medical data, provide diagnoses, and issue prescriptions, while groomers receive job order data to perform
grooming services. Payment confirmations and billing information are also processed through the staff to ensure proper transaction handling.

Additionally, the system includes SMS reminders for vaccination schedules, laboratory check-ups, and follow-up visits, helping maintain consistent communication with clients. All relevant data, including client, pet, and medical records, are stored in logbooks for documentation and future reference.
Overall, the process highlights a structured but staff-dependent workflow

information flows between multiple entities. While it organizes clinic operations, it still relies heavily on manual coordination and data handling, which may lead to delays, data inconsistencies, and inefficiencies without a fully automated system.

The Context Diagram Level 0 illustrates the physical design and external interactions of the AI-Integrated Management System with SMS Reminder for House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic and Grooming Salon. This diagram presents the key external entities that interact with the system and the primary data flows exchanged between them, highlighting how information is processed and distributed across users.

The main external entities in the system are the Owner, Admin (Veterinarian), Staff, and Client, each with specific roles and responsibilities. The Owner interacts with the system primarily for monitoring and decision-making purposes. Through the system, the owner can access client data, pet medical data, inventory information, and generated reports. This enables efficient oversight of clinic operations and supports informed managerial decisions.

(Veterinarian) plays a critical role in managing medical-related data within the system. The veterinarian inputs and retrieves pet medical records, prescriptions, and client and pet data. Additionally, the admin can access inventory details, ensuring that medical supplies and resources are properly monitored and managed. This interaction ensures accurate diagnosis documentation and proper treatment recording within the system.

The Staff serves as the primary encoder and handler of operational data. They input and manage client data, pet data, medical records, and inventory information. Staff members are also responsible for scheduling key services such as vaccination appointments, laboratory check-ups, and follow-up visits. These schedules are then processed by the system to automate reminders and maintain organized clinic workflows.

The Client interacts with the system indirectly through SMS reminders. The system sends automated notifications regarding vaccination schedules, laboratory check-ups, and follow-up visits, helping clients stay informed and maintain their pets’ health routines. However, clients do not have direct access to the system interface.

Overall, this context diagram provides a high-level representation of how the system functions within the clinic’s environment, emphasizing the flow of information between users and the system. It highlights the centralized role of the AI-integrated system in managing data, automating reminders, and supporting clinic operations, thereby promoting efficiency, accuracy, and improved service delivery.

The DFD Level 1 (Physical Design) presents the detailed flow of data within the Veterinary Management System. It shows the major processes, external entities, data stores, and how information moves between them. This level focuses on the actual implementation, including system users, clients, and database tables.
The system involves two main external entities:
The system user (veterinary staff or administrator) interacts with the system to manage client records, pet information, prescriptions, inventory, and clinical data.
Clients are pet owners who receive notifications such as SMS reminders related to prescriptions and pet care.
The system uses several database tables to store information:
Stores staff login credentials and user account data.
Stores pet-related information such as medical history and identification details.
Stores client or pet owner information.
Stores clinical and medical records of pets.
Stores inventory data such as medicines and supplies.
Stores reminder data used for sending SMS notifications.
There are six major processes in the system:
This process verifies login credentials entered by the system user.
The system user enters login credentials.
The system verifies credentials using the
Access is granted and returned to the system user.
To allow secure access to the system.
This process manages client and pet records.
The system user inputs client and pet data.
The system stores and retrieves data from
Updated client and pet data are returned to the system user.
To maintain accurate records of clients and their pets.
This process handles clinical and medical records of pets.
The system user inputs clinical details and pet data.
The system stores and retrieves records from
Clinical record details are returned to the system user.
To track and manage pet medical histories and treatments.
The system user inputs prescription and clinical details.
The system stores and retrieves data from
Prescription details are returned and can be used for reminders.
To manage and monitor prescribed medications for pets.
This process manages medical inventory such as medicines and supplies.
The system user inputs inventory data.
The system stores and retrieves data from Inventory (D6).
Inventory details are updated and returned to the system user.
To ensure proper tracking and availability of medical supplies.
This process handles sending reminders to clients.
The system retrieves reminder data from SMS reminder (D7).
Prescription and client data are used to generate reminders.
SMS reminders are sent to the client.
To notify clients about prescriptions, schedules, or follow-ups.
Role of System User in the System
The System User serves as the central controller of the system. The system user:
The DFD Level 1 shows how the system
Stores and retrieves client and pet data
Sends automated SMS reminders to clients
Maintains organized data using multiple database tables
This ensures efficient veterinary service management, accurate medical record keeping, proper inventory tracking, and effective communication between the clinic and clients

The Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) for the House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming database illustrates the logical structure of how patient records, prescriptions, appointments, staff roles, and related data are organized and interconnected. This design reflects the clinic’s operational workflow and addresses the challenges of their previous manual, paper-based system. The core entities include Pet Owner Information and Pet Information, which store essential client and animal details. Each pet is linked to its owner, establishing a clear relationship that supports efficient data retrieval. The Clinical Record entity captures visit

details, including the purpose of visits such as grooming, check-ups, surgeries, or lab tests, and is linked to the specific pet and the staff member responsible for creating the record. This entity is central,
to Prescription Records, where all medication details and dosage instructions are securely stored and linked to the corresponding clinical visit.

Supporting entities such as SMS Reminder and Inventory enable automated notifications and supply management, respectively. The SMS Reminder entity tracks scheduled follow-ups, vaccinations, laboratory check-ups, and grooming reminders to reduce missed appointments and enhance client communication. The Staff entity incorporates roles like staff, veterinarian admin, and super admin, managing access control and permissions within the system. Additionally, Forms such as waivers and consent documents are linked to pets, ensuring all necessary legal and procedural documentation is digitally stored and easily accessible. This structured database schema is designed to improve workflow efficiency, reduce data loss risks, and support automated processes in line with the clinic’s objectives to modernize patient care and service delivery.
